title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to UML and Unified Process. UML diagrams, Types of
relationships, Use case modelling: Actors and Use cases, Use case
specification, Actor generalization, Use case generalization. Objects and
classes, Relationships, Inheritance and Polymorphism, Packages
Case study: Class diagram for University Library management software

title: Module 2
content:
Use case realization: Interactions, Types of messaging – synchronous and
asynchronous, Sequence diagrams, Communication diagrams, Interaction
occurrences. Activity diagrams: Activity semantics, activity partitions,
sending signals and accepting events, Interaction overview diagrams.

title: Module 3
content:
Design: Design workflow, well-formed design classes, Refining analysis
relationships. Interfaces and components. State machine diagrams,
Composite states, sub machine states. Component diagram and Deployment
diagram.
Software design: Software architecture and its importance, Software
architecture patterns: Component and Connector, Layered, Repository,
Client-Server, Publish-Subscribe,

title: Module 4
content:
Software pattern: Model View Controller, Creational Design    Pattern types
– Factory method, Abstract Factory method, Singleton method, Prototype
method, Builder method. Structural Design Pattern and its types – Adapter,
Bridge, Proxy, Composite, Decorator, Façade, Flyweight.
